content.full_text:
Law Offices of

OLSON
CONNELLY
HAGEL
FONG &
LEIDEIGH

October 30, 1992

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

RE: REQUEST FOR ADVICE

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This firm has been authorized by "People Against Poker, No on P" and Ms. Kathy A. Amato, the treasurer, to request advice on their behalf pursuant to Government Code section 83114(b) concerning the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.

"People Against Poker, No on P" is a committee primarily formed to oppose the passage of a local ballot proposition in the City of Hawthorne. The committee is not controlled or sponsored within the meaning of the Act. The question has arisen whether one of the contributors to the committee is a sponsor under Government Code section 82048.7(b)(1); this contributor would not otherwise be a sponsor under this section.

Section 82048.7(b)(1) provides that a person sponsors a committee if "[t]he committee receives 80 percent or more of its contributions from the person or its members, officers, employees, or shareholders." The contributor in question is a committee called Californians for Fair Business Practices, ID # 890799. This committee contributed $10,000 to the committee on September 9, 1992. As of September 30, 1992, the end of the reporting period, this contribution represented approximately 94% of the contributions to date. However, it was anticipated at that time that additional contributions would be received by the committee.

As of the end of the second preelection reporting period, during which time additional contributions were collected, the Californians for Fair Business Practices's $10,000 contribution now represented

LANCE H. OLSON
BRUCE J. HAGEL
LEROY Y. FONG
ROBERT E. LEIDEIGH
GEORGE WATERS
DIANE M. FISHBURN

LLOYD G. CONNELLY

CapitolBank Center
300 Capitol Mall, Suite 350
Sacramento, CA 95814

Telephone (916) 442-2952
Facsimile (916) 442-1280


Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
October 30, 1992
Page 2

approximately 59% of the committee's total contributions to date.
This percentage is even smaller if the contributions received
during the late reporting period are taken into account. It is
not anticipated that the Californians for Fair Business Practices
will contribute additional monies to the committee.

Given these circumstances, is Californians for Fair Business
Practices a sponsor of the committee? It is our view that
Californians for Fair Business Practices is not a sponsor of the
committee. The contributor's overall participation in the
committee was limited to the one contribution which did not
constitute 80% or more of the committee's total contributions and
was intended to assist the committee in its start-up of the
campaign.

Thank you for your time and attention to this matter.

Very truly yours,

OLSON, CONNELLY, HAGEL, FONG & LEIDIGH

LANCE H. OLSON

LHO:jlh
cc: Hawthorne City Clerk
